notes: v1.7.0 — POST-PIVOT ORPHAN SWEEP (micro-cut): three semantic orphans the Master Pivot left behind, each ~one conditional, gating/empty-state/resolver only — NO data/machinery/CSS changes, legacy EDP deployments keep ALL behavior. NOTE: web-Claude authored this spec as 'v1.6.98', but v1.6.98 (Troubleshoot Assistant) + v1.6.99 (HUD redesign) already shipped, so this ships as v1.7.0 — version collision resolved (same pattern as v1.6.97-spec→v1.6.99). ITEM 1 — 'AI REVIEW — Validate EDP Parse' button gated to legacy deployments: on Master-scoped deps, edpParsed holds snapshot-derived racks from a deterministic spreadsheet read — there is no parse to validate, so the 90s AI call (Worker quota) returned noise and mis-taught users where their data came from. Now rendered ONLY when !dep.scope (same key as the backward-compat banner); Master-scoped (dep.scope.source==='master') never shows it. Belt-and-suspenders: deploy_aiReview early-returns with a toast ('AI Review is not applicable to Master-scoped deployments') if invoked with a scoped dep (guards stale DOM). ITEM 2 — PORTMAP/Optic-Score VERIFY sub-tab stops rendering a misleading hollow state on Master-scoped deps: Master-scoped deployments never carry edpParsed.portMaps, so the verify pane's idle hint used to say 'Load an EDP first... use INTAKE' (INTAKE was retired in the pivot). Now, when the active dep is Master-scoped, the idle state reads an honest line — 'Port maps come from vendor EDPs — attach one under Reconcile, or see Device Connections for Master cabling' — pointing at the two real places the data lives; legacy deps with real portMaps render unchanged. OUT of scope (roadmap, not this cut): feeding PORTMAP from the Master snapshot's cable edges (cablesOut/In). ITEM 3 — Mission Brief (brief_generate) now resolves the active deployment via the shared nowtab_resolveDep() (getActive-first, most-recent-active fallback) instead of its own private 'sort active/paused by updated, take head' path — the exact independent-resolution pattern v1.6.95 outlawed; counting stays on the already-shared deploy_computeDeployRollup. So the Brief greets with the SAME deployment the NOW tab shows. VERIFIED: node --check 2/2 inline scripts + sw.js, version.json valid, block-0 braces UNCHANGED at 2565 (no CSS this ship), three stamps v1.7.0. Built in Claude Code on 6cc265b (v1.6.99) per the one-builder rule; web-Claude post-merge parity review pending; operator walkthrough pending (Master-scoped dep: no AI REVIEW button + honest PORTMAP empty-state; legacy dep: both unchanged; Mission Brief matches NOW tab).
